title: teucrium polium subsp. eriocephalum
intro: Teucrium polium subsp. eriocephalum, also known as the Syrian germander, is a plant species that belongs to the Lamiaceae family. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Teucrium polium subsp. eriocephalum include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating digestive issues, as well as its potential in reducing the risk of certain diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in traditional medicine in the Middle East and North Africa.
